title: How To Prevent Dampness From The Wall!
shortDescription: Condensation acts as the major cause of dampness in many homes. Majorly the kitchens, the bathrooms and bedrooms are the most affected areas caused by the day to day house old activities like cooking, washing and drying clothes.
detailedDescription:
How To Prevent Dampness From The Wall!

Dampness of the walls is caused by excess moisture either from internal sources or external intrusions. Internal sources include kitchens and air conditioning while external factors include rains and lack of proper insulation which reduce the strength between bricks and plaster.

Causes of Dampness

Condensation is the major cause of dampness in many homes especially in kitchens, bathrooms and bedrooms due to daily activities like cooking, washing and drying clothes.

Poor ventilation increases condensation. Adequate airflow ensures proper warmth flow and removes moisture from the air.

Civil flaws like improper plumbing fixation, inadequate gutters and high groundwater levels also cause dampness.

Prevention of Dampness

Cracks near doors and window frames allow moisture seepage which can be treated by filling cracks with putty and repainting.

Waterproofing the terrace is an ideal solution to avoid dampness which otherwise affects wood works and paintings.

Using first quality bricks, correct mortar mix and damp proof course above ground level helps prevent dampness.

Architect Akila
seoDescription: How to prevent dampness from wall.

title: What Is The Best Material For An Interior Design?
shortDescription: Plywood is a good and favorite material for interior design as it’s very light and can easily be customized. It can be applied to not only furniture but also to ceilings, walls, and partitions. Plywood gives a contemporary look to the design and has a great appeal.
detailedDescription:
What Is The Best Material For An Interior Design?

Plywood is one of the most preferred materials for interior design because it is lightweight, flexible, durable and budget friendly.

Plywood Definition

Plywood is manufactured by bonding thin veneer sheets with resin under pressure to create a strong and flexible flat board.

Plywood as a Material

It is economical, recyclable, easy to install and has remained popular for decades due to its strength and versatility.

Plywood for Interior Design

Plywood is ideal for walls, partitions, furniture, kitchens, bedrooms, bathrooms and cabinets while complementing steel and concrete finishes.

Best Plywoods Available

The most commonly used plywoods are MR Ply, BWR/BWP Ply and Marine Ply.

Moisture Resistant Plywood

Commercial MR Ply offers moderate moisture resistance and is widely used for interior furnishing.

BWR & BWP Plywood

These waterproof plywoods are suitable for kitchens, bathrooms, exteriors and wet areas.

Marine Ply

Marine plywood is highly waterproof and used in areas with continuous water exposure.

Plywood is eco-friendly, warp resistant and long lasting making it one of the best materials for modern interior design.

Architect Raakesh Natarajan

title: How is interior wall thickness determined?
shortDescription: When it carries plumbing or Electrical lines the thickness should be 150mm minimum or 230mm maximum as regular wall and if it is a load bearing supporting wall with utilities then it should be definitely 230mm.
detailedDescription:
How is interior wall thickness determined?

Interior wall thickness depends on wall function, acoustic requirements and construction material.

Wall Function

Partition walls are usually 125mm thick including plaster, while walls carrying plumbing and electrical services require 150mm to 230mm thickness.

Acoustic Requirements

Cavity walls, drywall and timber partitions range from 125mm to 150mm providing sound insulation.

Material Used

Brick, AAC block and concrete blocks typically require minimum 100mm thickness for partitions.

Calculation

Wall thickness can be measured using door jamb dimensions and ID–OD differences.

Suggested Thickness

Rammed Earth: 375–450mm, Adobe Brick: 300mm, Poured Concrete: 125–200mm, Reinforced Concrete: 125mm, Stone: 250mm, Cement Blocks: 100–150mm.

Architect Sabarinathan
seoDescription: How is wall thickness determined.

title: Tips to choose Accessories & Appliances for your Modular Kitchen
shortDescription: The first thing that comes to our mind with respect to kitchen accessories or appliances is the utility they provide to our modular kitchen. Therefore it is first advised to understand the utilities they provide along with its aesthetics.
detailedDescription:
Tips to Choose Accessories & Appliances for Your Modular Kitchen

When choosing accessories and appliances for a modular kitchen, functionality should be given priority along with aesthetics. Selecting energy-efficient appliances with higher star ratings ensures reduced power consumption and long-term savings while improving kitchen performance.

Tips While Choosing Appliances
Cooktops & Ovens

Choose free-standing or built-in ovens with automatic programs, temperature memory settings and multiple shelf options for ease of cooking and flexibility.

Hobs

Induction hobs are highly energy efficient and safe, while gas-on-glass hobs provide instant heating with stylish designs.

Microwaves

Compact built-in microwaves and steam ovens improve cooking versatility and save kitchen space.

Dishwashers

Modern dishwashers use less water than manual washing and feature water-saving and energy-saving programs.

Tips While Choosing Accessories
Cutlery Organisers

Drawer organisers maintain order and quick accessibility. Avoid placing cutlery drawers directly below the cooktop.

Pull-Out Baskets

Use telescopic pull-out baskets for deep and shallow storage. Avoid overloading to maintain smooth functioning.

Bottle Pull-Outs

Bottle pull-outs efficiently utilize narrow spaces and provide easy access to oils, sauces and seasonings.

Architect Neshma
seoDescription: Tips to choose accessories and appliances for modular kitchen.

title: How To Decor Your Home In Phases – With Time Intervals
shortDescription: Moving into a new home can be one of life’s greatest joys, but it can also be a time of uncertainty, especially when it comes to decorating. How do you make your space look its best while reflecting your personal sense of style? Do it well and you’ll end up with a comfortable, happy home.
detailedDescription:
How To Decor Your Home In Phases – With Time Intervals

Decorating a new home can be exciting yet overwhelming. By following a phased interior design approach used by professionals, you can create a cohesive, comfortable and stylish home without rushing decisions.

Where To Start

Think of your home design in layers and complete one phase at a time, starting from core structure and ending with décor accessories.

1. Choose Priority Rooms

Begin with the rooms you use the most. Take proper measurements to plan furniture placement accurately.

2. Decide Existing Furniture

Select furniture pieces you want to retain and design the rest of the décor around them.

3. Create Floor Plans

Design a floor layout that allows easy movement and proportional furniture placement.

4. Pick Your Color Palette

Build your color scheme from rugs, artworks or colors you love the most.

5. Paint

Painting instantly personalizes your space. Accent walls and neutral tones create character without overcommitment.

6. Control Lighting

Layered lighting ensures mood control using natural light, overhead lights, lamps and curtains.

7. Finish With Art & Accessories

Complete your interiors with artwork, décor objects and accessories that match your style.

Architect Raghul
seoDescription: Moving into a new home.

1. Tiered Shelves

Utilize vertical wall space by stacking multiple layers of open shelves.

2. Fake Breakfast Counter

Install a slim wood countertop against the wall and add bar stools to create a breakfast nook.

3. Drawer Storage

Reduce countertop clutter by assigning drawers for produce and essentials.

4. Minimalist Design

Simple colors, clean shelving and clutter-free design visually enlarge your kitchen.

5. Maximalist Style

Bold patterns and accessories add warmth and personality to compact kitchens.

6. Extra Storage Islands

Use compact islands with open shelves for triple functionality.

7. Linear Kitchen Layout

Horizontal tiles and straight layouts visually widen narrow spaces.

8. Colorful Rugs

Add texture and color with vibrant rugs.

9. Hidden Reminder Station

Use inside cabinet doors for grocery notes and reminders.

10. Mirrors

Mirrors reflect light and make kitchens look larger and brighter.

Over-sink racks add storage without occupying counter space.

12. Decorative Displays

Use attractive utensils and crockery as décor.

title: 10 Questions, You must ask your Interior Designer
shortDescription: When basic requirements are met then a house can become a functional dwelling however, to make it a home one would have to exclusively customize it according to their needs.
detailedDescription:
10 Questions, You Must Ask Your Interior Designer

Choosing the right interior designer is a crucial step in transforming your house into a functional and comfortable home. Asking the right questions beforehand can help you avoid costly mistakes and ensure transparency, durability and long-term satisfaction.

Essential Questions to Ask

1. Are you an Interior Decorator or an Interior Designer? Understand whether they modify existing spaces or redesign spaces from scratch.

2. Are you offering unnecessary volumes at cheap prices? Excess woodwork and low-grade materials compromise long-term durability.

3. Are you a seller or a manufacturer? Choose firms with in-house manufacturing and automation standards.

4. What customization options do you offer? Advanced machinery ensures better personalization.

5. How experienced and reputable is your company? Longevity, innovations and regional reputation matter.

6. Do you handle projects similar to mine? Choose specialists for your scale and budget.

7. Can you share completed project references? Visit nearby completed projects for assurance.

8. What is your project timeline and service clarity? Look for minimum 10-year guarantee and annual service.

9. How transparent is your pricing? Clear material specs, tax transparency and EMI support are signs of a reliable company.

10. Are your designs future-ready? Technology, design software and execution standards should be modern.

11. Do you offer support beyond project scope? Good firms assist with lighting, plumbing, appliances and contractor guidance.

Architect Vignesh Kumar
seoDescription: Questions to ask your interior designer.

title: Ideas to Brighten up a dark Kitchen
shortDescription: Kitchen is one of the most important and integral part of any Residential Interior Design Projects. This is the place where sharp objects like knifes and cutters are used, apart from this people also come into contact with fire here....
detailedDescription:
Ideas to Brighten Up a Dark Kitchen

The kitchen is one of the most important spaces in any home. It is an area where sharp tools and fire are used regularly, making proper lighting and ventilation essential. A well-lit kitchen not only improves safety but also enhances the overall aesthetics and functionality of your interiors. Here are a few simple ideas to brighten up a dark kitchen with minimal changes.

1. Mirror on the Wall

Mirrors reflect light effectively and create an illusion of a brighter and more spacious kitchen. Installing mirrors tastefully can completely transform the look of a dull kitchen.

2. Under-Cabinet Lighting

Installing lighting below wall cabinets is an excellent way to illuminate work areas. Options such as LED strips, puck lights and fluorescent bars help make cooking spaces brighter and safer.

3. The Art of Contrasting

Create a focal point by highlighting a wall, backsplash or dado tiles in contrasting colours or textures. This adds visual interest and brightens the overall kitchen ambience.

4. Add Metallic Touches

Metallic elements such as stainless-steel sinks, shiny appliances and reflective tiles help bounce light around the kitchen, making it appear brighter and more elegant.

These simple solutions can be implemented in both new and renovated kitchens. Adding extra lighting through lamps and high-power bulbs further improves visibility and functionality.

Architect Anulekha
seoDescription: Ideas to brighten up a dark kitchen.

title: How to design your bedroom ideal for the peaceful sleep using interior
shortDescription: The bedroom is the perfect place at home for relaxation and refuvenation.it has an important role to design a bedroom with suitable interior which is includes color, pattern, texture and furniture.
detailedDescription:
How to Design Your Bedroom for Peaceful Sleep Using Interior Design

Your bedroom is the most important space in your home for relaxation and rejuvenation. A well-designed bedroom promotes healthy sleep, reduces stress and enhances overall wellbeing. Choosing the right colours, lighting, textures and furniture plays a vital role in creating a calm and soothing environment.

Install Stylish and Organised Furniture

Cot: Choose a bed that is proportional to your room size. A well-designed headboard with light accents and storage niches is ideal for small bedrooms. Wall-mounted bedside shelves offer minimalistic storage while saving floor space. Placing a soft rug beneath the cot adds warmth and comfort.

Corner Units: Utilise corners with open shelves or seating areas to create a cosy nook for relaxing, reading or conversations.

Artistic Wall: Dedicate a wall for a dresser or artistic feature. You can install a full-length mirror with storage or enhance the wall with textured paint or wallpaper along with a chest of drawers.

Wardrobe Options: Sliding wardrobes are ideal for compact rooms, built-in wardrobes work best for larger bedrooms, and walk-in wardrobes offer privacy and luxury.

Choose Calming Colours & Lighting

Colours and lighting significantly influence sleep quality. Dark shades suit light sleepers, while warm soft tones benefit early risers. Neutral palettes with accent walls create a peaceful ambience.

Maximise natural daylight wherever possible. Complement it with soft artificial lighting such as wall sconces, floor lamps, hanging lights and ceiling lights to highlight artworks and architectural elements.

Accent colours, lighting, artworks and smart furniture choices personalise your bedroom and create a healthy, relaxing sleep environment.

Architect Priyatharshini
seoDescription: Design your bedroom for peaceful sleep.

title: Interior Design Ideas for Latest Pooja Room Design
shortDescription: Pooja is the adobe of God present in our homes. The Pooja room/Mandir should always be placed in the North, East or the Northeast side of the home. Pooja space is the most important part of our house, as it keeps our house filled with positive energies.
detailedDescription:
Interior Design Ideas for the Latest Pooja Room Designs

A pooja room is the sacred space in every home where peace, positivity and spirituality come together. Traditionally, the pooja area should be placed in the North, East or North-East direction to attract positive energies. Regardless of faith, almost every household dedicates a corner for prayer, making it one of the most important spaces in the home.

For a perfectly designed pooja room, the arrangement of mandir units, idols, accessories and furniture must be done thoughtfully. Colours, materials and lighting should blend harmoniously to create a calm and meditative ambience.

Here are some simple and stylish ideas to design a modern pooja room:

1. The Charm of Carved Elements

Carved wooden pooja units bring timeless elegance and traditional charm. Wood adds warmth, beauty and a sense of heritage to the space. Intricate carvings inspired by temple architecture create a divine atmosphere. For compact homes, small carved mandir cabinets work beautifully, while larger homes can opt for grand wooden units.

2. The Beauty of Traceries (Jaalis)

Jaalis or decorative traceries add depth, texture and drama to pooja rooms. They can be used as partitions, backdrops or framed panels. With modern CNC and laser-cutting techniques, jaalis can be designed in wood, MDF or metal. Adding concealed lighting behind jaalis enhances their visual appeal.

3. Add Colour with Rangoli

Decorate your pooja area with floral rangoli designs, floating flowers and scented candles placed in decorative bowls of water. This instantly brings vibrancy, freshness and festive charm to your mandir entrance.

These design ideas will not only enhance the beauty of your pooja room but also fill your home with peace, positivity and spiritual energy.

title: How to choose the best interior designer?

How to Choose the Best Interior Designer

Choosing the right interior designer is one of the most important decisions when planning your home interiors. A good designer not only understands your requirements but also provides practical, creative and budget-friendly solutions.

Steps to Choose the Right Interior Designer
1. Identify Your Preferred Design Style

Explore different design styles through websites, magazines and social platforms. Understanding your preferred style helps you shortlist designers whose signature style matches your expectations.

2. Review Portfolios

Check the designer’s previous projects to understand their creativity, quality of work and design execution. This gives you a clear idea of what you can expect.

3. Set a Budget

Fix a realistic budget before meeting designers. Some charge fixed fees while others follow hourly or project-based pricing. A defined budget helps narrow your choices.

4. Visit the Studio

Meeting designers in person at their studio builds trust and helps you understand their working style, team strength and design approach.

5. Ask Relevant Questions

Discuss timelines, materials, guarantees, services, after-sales support and previous client references. Note down important points for later comparison.

6. Keep an Open Mind

Professional designers may suggest smarter alternatives that improve functionality and aesthetics. Being open to ideas often results in better designs.

7. Compare Options Carefully

Compare quotations, timelines, materials and service commitments. The cheapest option is not always the best—quality and service matter more.

8. Sign a Clear Contract

Before starting, ensure all terms, timelines, specifications, guarantees and payment schedules are clearly mentioned in the agreement.

With proper planning, transparent communication and the right designer, your interior project becomes smooth, efficient and highly rewarding.

Materials Used in Modular Kitchens

• HDHMR (High Density High Moisture Resistant)

Contemporary: Sleek, minimal and highly functional.

Chic: Trendy and fashionable designs.

Easy Cleaning & Repairs

Easy to maintain with simple wiping and minimal effort.

Durability

Using BWP and HDHMR ensures long-lasting performance.

Maintenance Tips for Modular Kitchens

• Service kitchen hardware once a year.

• Clean appliances and surfaces regularly.

• Use soft scrubbers to avoid scratches.

• Use baking soda to maintain shine.

• Clean refrigerators twice a month.

• Clean chimneys twice a year.

Ideas to Incorporate a Veg & Fruit Garden in Your Home Interior Design

Creating an indoor vegetable and fruit garden allows you to enjoy fresh organic produce while enhancing your home interiors. With proper planning, even small spaces can be transformed into green, refreshing corners.

Start with pots that have proper drainage holes and use specially formulated indoor potting soil. Some plants may require deeper containers to allow roots to grow well.

Place your garden near a sunny window. Vegetables require at least 4–6 hours of sunlight daily, while fruits need 8–10 hours. A cool mist humidifier can help maintain humidity levels and prevent plants from drying out.

Common indoor-friendly vegetables and fruits include scallions, radishes, microgreens, tomatoes, potatoes, strawberries and spinach.

Unused spaces such as under staircases can be converted into compact gardens. Add decorative pebbles, rocks and flowering plants to enhance visual appeal. If sunlight is limited, install grow lights placed at least 2 feet above plants.

Vertical gardens are another excellent option. Ensure proper water drainage by placing water-tolerant plants in lower rows. Vegetables like lettuce, carrots, turnips and leafy greens grow well indoors and require minimal care.

Apart from visual appeal, indoor gardens improve air quality, add humidity and create a refreshing living environment.

Interior design is more than visual beauty — it is the art of transforming spaces into functional, comfortable and emotionally satisfying environments. From elegant ceiling chandeliers to decorative TV walls, everyone dreams of a personalized home that reflects their lifestyle and personality.

A house truly becomes a home when every corner is thoughtfully designed to support everyday activities. Organized storage, designated key holders, compartmentalized wardrobes and efficient layouts make daily living easier and stress-free.

Contrary to popular belief, interior design is not necessarily expensive. Minimalistic and smart design strategies can significantly improve usability without inflating budgets. By maximizing space efficiency and selecting appropriate décor styles, interiors can be both practical and visually appealing.

Professional interior designers help translate your lifestyle needs into tangible designs, ensuring every element serves a purpose while maintaining aesthetic harmony.

detailedDescription:
How to Choose the Right Interior Design Company

The quality and functionality of a building are as important as its aesthetics. Whether residential or commercial, interiors play a crucial role in enhancing comfort, accessibility and visual appeal. Choosing the right interior design company ensures that your space is both beautiful and functional.

1. Ease of Access

A professional interior company should be easy to reach, with clear contact details, office location and an informative website or online presence.

2. Range of Services

Understand the full range of services offered — from modular kitchens and wardrobes to false ceilings, wallpapers, partitions and complete turnkey interiors.

3. Portfolio & Experience

Review completed projects to understand the company’s design quality, finish standards and style versatility.

4. Cost Transparency & Customization

Ensure that the pricing is transparent and that customization options fit your budget and lifestyle needs.

5. Creative Design Solutions

A reliable company should offer multiple design concepts that are both practical and visually appealing.

6. Dispute-Free Service

The company must follow a clear process from design approval to installation, ensuring smooth execution without conflicts.

title: INTERIOR DESIGN IDEAS FOR YOUR DREAM HOME
shortDescription: The first and foremost thing to do before remodeling any type of Home Interiors is to choose the right type of color palette. Many famous designers come up with a color combination for the entire house and then detail them out in different areas and rooms......
detailedDescription:
Interior Design Ideas for Your Dream Home

Designing your dream home can feel overwhelming, especially if you are not a professional designer. However, with the right ideas and inspiration, anyone can create an elegant and functional living space. Here are some simple and effective interior design ideas to help you design your perfect home.

Creating Dynamic Spaces with Colour Patterns

Choosing the right colour palette is the first step in home interior design. Bedrooms should feature soft and neutral tones to promote relaxation, while brighter colours can be used in common areas for energy and vibrancy.

Mix and Match Colours

Contrasting colour combinations, such as pairing bold shades with white, help create balanced and visually pleasing spaces.

Use Wallpapers to Brighten Interiors

Wallpapers are an easy way to enhance lobbies, passages and storage spaces, instantly adding life and personality.

Play with Textures

Combining matte and glossy textures creates depth, contrast and an interesting interplay of light and shadow within your home.

High Furniture for Higher Ceilings

Using tall furniture in low-ceiling rooms helps visually increase ceiling height, making rooms feel more spacious and open.

These simple interior design tips can transform your house into a beautiful and character-filled home.

Architect Anulekha

detailedDescription:
Tips for Choosing the Best Interior Designer for Your Dream Home

Choosing the right interior designer is the first and most important step in transforming your house into your dream home. Here are some practical tips to help you select a designer who matches your style, budget and expectations.

Design Style

Explore designers online and shortlist those whose portfolios match your aesthetic. A good designer should offer end-to-end services and help convert your ideas into functional, beautiful interiors.

Quality and Finish

Quality and finish go hand in hand. Always visit showrooms, experience centres and completed projects to understand the durability and real application of materials.

Own Manufacturing Unit

Design firms with their own factories ensure better quality control, faster delivery and competitive pricing compared to those depending on external vendors.

Prompt Delivery

Timely delivery is crucial. Speak to previous clients to verify timelines and overall satisfaction.

Price vs Value

Compare proposals fairly by considering quality, service and warranty rather than price alone. Value matters more than cost.

Choosing the right designer is a long-term investment that brings comfort, functionality and happiness to your home.

title: WHY EXPERTS ARE CRUCIAL TO DESIGN AND BUILD “DREAM HOMES”?
shortDescription: An Architect’s job is to design a house prior to construction. Construction of a house is quiet a challenging endeavor and Architects can ease this process by taking charge of the project from the design to the execution phase......
detailedDescription:
Why Experts Are Crucial to Design and Build Dream Homes

Owning a dream home is a goal for many, but turning that dream into reality requires careful planning and the right professionals. Hiring experts ensures quality construction, functional design and long-term value.

Role of an Architect

Architects design efficient, sustainable and cost-effective homes. They manage the design process, understand client requirements, prepare drawings and ensure the space is optimized for comfort, aesthetics and practicality.

Role of a Civil Engineer

Civil engineers ensure the structural integrity of the building. They review designs, choose suitable construction materials, follow building codes and make sure the structure is safe and durable.

Role of an Interior Designer

Interior designers shape the character of the home by planning functional layouts, selecting materials, designing customized furniture and enhancing the visual appeal of each space.

Together, these experts transform a building into a comfortable, beautiful and long-lasting home.

title: WHY HIRE AN INTERIOR DESIGNER INSTEAD OF A CARPENTER???
shortDescription: It’s not really acceptable to start looking for factory – finished woodwork for interiors and fancy displays of models when we have always preferred carpenters who come home and work for days and months together cutting and pasting the materials....
detailedDescription:
Why Hire an Interior Designer Instead of a Carpenter?

When furnishing a new home, most homeowners debate between hiring a local carpenter or a professional interior designer. While carpenters have traditionally handled woodwork, modern factory-finished interiors designed by professionals offer better quality, durability and visual appeal.

Pre-Planned Design Concepts

Interior designers provide detailed 2D and 3D designs before production begins, allowing homeowners to visualize the final outcome clearly. This eliminates uncertainty and reduces errors, unlike on-site carpentry where the end result is visible only after completion.

Superior Finish & Flexibility

Factory-finished modular interiors are precisely manufactured and assembled, ensuring consistent quality. These units can also be dismantled and relocated easily, unlike permanently fixed carpenter-made units.

Cost vs Value

Though professional interiors may seem costlier initially, they offer better long-term value, durability and reduced maintenance. Investing in quality materials ensures longevity and prevents costly repairs later.

Careful evaluation of finishes, materials and factory processes will help you make the right decision. Quality always comes with value, making professional interior designers a smart investment for your dream home.

Architect Sowmithra

title: WHY CHENNAI??

detailedDescription:
Why Chennai?

Chennai has emerged as one of India’s fastest growing real estate destinations owing to its rapid infrastructural development, booming economy and rich cultural heritage. Originally a small fishing hamlet known as Madraspatnam, the city began transforming into a major trade and administrative hub with the establishment of Fort St. George in 1644.

Over centuries, Chennai evolved into a metropolitan powerhouse covering over 8,800 square kilometers, becoming a preferred destination for residential and commercial developments.

How Chennai Became India’s Real Estate Hotspot

Post-independence, Chennai became one of India’s four major metropolitan cities. Today, it houses major industries such as IT, automobiles, banking, construction, healthcare, petrochemicals, textiles and entertainment, driving continuous migration and real estate demand.

The city is widely recognized for its safety, livability and infrastructure, being rated among India’s safest cities and selected under the Smart Cities Mission.

Our Manufacturing Unit

With increasing new developments and renovation projects, Chennai’s interior design and manufacturing sectors are growing rapidly. This positive growth environment makes Chennai a global hub for innovative residential and commercial interior solutions.
